mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and deteriorating corporate earnings prospects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following a 0.2pp UNRATE increase, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and builder confidence, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both exceed their 12-month moving averages, with a walk-forward Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects tightening capacity utilization and rising corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where INDPRO month-over-month growth exceeds 0.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during inflationary spikes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stagnant or rising federal funds rate forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression of 60-day returns, or positive returns dominate when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate consumer spending capacity and supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, with a mean directional accuracy below 48%.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms robust economic expansion and earnings momentum, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 2.0%, or the signal exhibits negative predictive power during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment indicates a healthy labor-market-driven supply expansion, supporting corporate profitability and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 60-day horizons, or the interaction effect reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.
